---
title: "A.9 NIST + FICAM Cross-Walk — UIAO Substrate Alignment with Federal ICAM Standards"
subtitle: "How UIAO substrate components map to NIST IR 8011, NIST SP 800-157, NIST SP 800-63 series, and the FICAM Architecture v3"
doc-type: compliance-assessment
audience: [Customer, Assessor, Architect, Sponsor]
classification: Controlled
boundary: GCC-Moderate
author: "UIAO Canon Engine"
date: "May 2026"
status: Draft
canon-source: "src/uiao/canon/substrate-manifest.yaml"
canon-reference:
  - "docs/customer-documents/whitepapers/federal-ssot-alignment.qmd"
  - "docs/customer-documents/whitepapers/federal-hrit-productization.qmd"
  - "docs/customer-documents/compliance/boundary-authorization/B1-1-gcc-moderate-three-way-conflict.qmd"
  - "docs/customer-documents/orgpath-narrative/07a-uiao-beneath-the-azure-ssot-stack.qmd"
  - "docs/customer-documents/case-studies/reference-deployment-opm-hrit-to-entra.qmd"
  - "docs/docs/16_DriftDetectionStandard.qmd"
  - "src/uiao/modernization/directory-migration/README.md"
  - "src/uiao/canon/specs/Spec2-D1.1-CanonicalHRAttributeSchema.md"
repo-path: docs/customer-documents/compliance/federal-mandates/nist-icam-crosswalk.qmd
pillar: compliance
sub-category: A
leaf: A.9
created-at: 2026-05-11
updated-at: 2026-05-12
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
  docx:
    toc: true
---

**CONTROLLED**

# A.9 NIST + FICAM Cross-Walk

## 1. Position

This page is the **substrate-to-standard cross-walk** federal agency RFP
writers, SI proposal authors, and 3PAO assessors search for when they
need a specific NIST or FICAM identifier next to a UIAO substrate
component. Every claim here is anchored to a specific canon document or
schema; speculative alignments are deliberately absent.

The standards covered:

| Standard | Scope | Relevance to UIAO |
|---|---|---|
| **NIST IR 8011** (Vol. 1–4) | Automation Support for Security Control Assessments | UIAO's evidence-as-byproduct claim. The substrate is the automation layer NIST IR 8011 contemplates for the identity domain. |
| **NIST SP 800-157** | Guidelines for Derived Personal Identity Verification (PIV) Credentials | The certificate-anchored substrate overlay. UIAO's `certificate-anchored: true` invariant is the substrate's instantiation of the derived-credential trust model. |
| **NIST SP 800-63 series** (800-63-3, -63A, -63B, -63C) | Digital Identity Guidelines — Identity Assurance Levels (IAL/AAL/FAL) | The COR schema encodes AAL per principal; drift findings surface conditions under which the encoded AAL is no longer continuously verified. **800-63A enrollment** is the upstream event the HRIT inbound provisioning pipeline (UIAO_136 / Spec 2) consumes — see §4.1. |
| **FICAM Architecture v3** | Federal Identity, Credential, and Access Management Reference Architecture | The federated-governance frame UIAO's six control planes implement. UIAO is the *substrate beneath* the FICAM Architecture, not an alternative to it. |

What this cross-walk does **not** do:

- Does not claim UIAO supersedes any of these standards. UIAO is the
  substrate that helps an agency operationalize them; it does not
  replace the standards or the agency's responsibility to interpret
  them.
- Does not assert FedRAMP authorization. UIAO is open-source canon and
  reference architecture; FedRAMP authorization belongs to the agency
  or service provider deploying it.
- Does not provide control implementation status. That is the agency's
  System Security Plan responsibility. The cross-walk identifies
  *which* UIAO components are relevant inputs to each control family,
  not whether any specific control is satisfied.

## 2. NIST IR 8011 — Automation Support for Security Control Assessments

NIST IR 8011 (multi-volume; Volume 1 is the methodology, Volumes 2–4
cover specific control sub-domains: hardware asset management, software
asset management, configuration settings management) defines the
automation primitives federal agencies are expected to use to convert
control assessment from a periodic-documentation exercise into a
continuous-evidence operation.

UIAO's substrate provides the automation primitives NIST IR 8011 names
in the **identity** sub-domain (a domain NIST IR 8011 does not yet
publish a volume for explicitly, but for which the methodology applies
directly):

| NIST IR 8011 capability | UIAO substrate component | Canon reference |
|---|---|---|
| Authoritative system inventory | OrgTree readiness bundle; COR schema | `src/uiao/schemas/orgtree-readiness/orgtree-readiness.schema.json` |
| Desired-state declaration | Canon documents under `src/uiao/canon/` with HMAC-signed provenance | `src/uiao/canon/substrate-manifest.yaml` |
| Actual-state observation | Modernization adapters (AD survey, Entra collector, Intune adapter, ScubaGear conformance) | `src/uiao/canon/modernization-registry.yaml`, `src/uiao/canon/adapter-registry.yaml` |
| Desired vs. actual comparison | Drift Engine; the five-class drift taxonomy | `docs/docs/16_DriftDetectionStandard.qmd` |
| Continuous-monitoring evidence | Evidence Bundle assembled deterministically per ADR-006 | `docs/docs/15_ProvenanceProfile.qmd`, `src/uiao/schemas/ksi/evidence-bundle.schema.json` |
| Defect identification + remediation tracking | Drift findings with severity model and remediation contract | `docs/docs/16_DriftDetectionStandard.qmd` §3–§4 |
| Assessor-consumable output | OSCAL-aligned assessment-results emission | `src/uiao/oscal/orgtree_evidence.py`, `src/uiao/generators/oscal.py` |

The substrate's `evidence-class: interval` adapter attribute (declared in
`adapter-registry.yaml`) is the operational form of NIST IR 8011's
"capability instance" concept — each adapter is a capability instance
whose interval-windowed findings accumulate into the assessment-results
output the methodology contemplates.

### 2.1 Identity sub-domain mapping

For the identity-specific sub-domain NIST IR 8011's volumes have not yet
published explicitly, UIAO's mapping of the methodology is:

- **Authoritative inventory** — the OrgTree registry + COR-schema
  principals (users, computers, service accounts, groups), each
  carrying an OrgPath that anchors organizational position
- **Desired state** — canon-declared adapter contracts under
  `src/uiao/modernization/directory-migration/adapters/` (8 contracts
  + the DM_090 SQL Server adapter)
- **Observation** — the AD survey adapter
  ([`active_directory/survey.py`](../../../../src/uiao/adapters/modernization/active_directory/survey.py)),
  Entra collector, and SCuBAGear conformance adapter
- **Drift output** — `DRIFT-IDENTITY`, `DRIFT-AUTHZ`,
  `DRIFT-PROVENANCE`, `DRIFT-SCHEMA`, `DRIFT-SEMANTIC` findings per
  the standard formalized in `16_DriftDetectionStandard.qmd`
- **Continuous-monitoring artifact** — the OrgTree readiness Evidence
  Bundle with HMAC-signed provenance + the phase-tagged
  `spn_inventory` artifact for migration-aware identity assessment

## 3. NIST SP 800-157 — Derived PIV Credentials

NIST SP 800-157 defines the cryptographic trust model under which a PIV
card's identity assertion is delegated to a mobile or workload-bound
credential. The substrate's `certificate-anchored: true` invariant is
the operational form of this model for adapter-emitted artifacts.

| NIST SP 800-157 concept | UIAO substrate component | Canon reference |
|---|---|---|
| Derived credential lifecycle | Adapter signing identity provisioning; credential rotation managed alongside the PKI adapter | `src/uiao/modernization/directory-migration/adapters/pki/pki-adapter-interface.md` |
| Cryptographic binding to the PIV chain of trust | Certificate-anchored adapter operations (every transaction tied to an identity certificate) | `src/uiao/canon/modernization-registry.yaml` (`certificate-anchored: true` invariant) |
| Authenticator assurance preservation | COR-encoded AAL per principal; `DRIFT-AUTHZ` surfaces drift between encoded AAL and observed credential state | `docs/docs/16_DriftDetectionStandard.qmd` §2 |
| Credential issuance audit trail | Provenance chain entries per credential operation, immutable | `docs/docs/15_ProvenanceProfile.qmd` |

The substrate does not *issue* derived credentials — that is the agency's
PIV-D issuing authority's responsibility. The substrate *governs* the
derived-credential operations: every adapter that uses a derived
credential records the credential's identity, the operation it
authorized, the canon document version the operation was made against,
and the resulting state hash in the provenance chain.

## 4. NIST SP 800-63 series — Identity Assurance

NIST SP 800-63-3 and its companion volumes (-63A enrollment, -63B
authenticator binding, -63C federation) define the three assurance
dimensions:

- **IAL** — Identity Assurance Level (how strongly was identity proofed)
- **AAL** — Authenticator Assurance Level (how strongly is the
  authenticator bound)
- **FAL** — Federation Assurance Level (how strongly are federated
  assertions protected)

UIAO encodes assurance levels as **canon attributes per principal** in
the COR schema. Drift findings surface conditions under which the
encoded assurance level is no longer continuously verified — which is
the precise failure mode 800-63 was written to prevent.

| 800-63 dimension | UIAO substrate component | Canon reference |
|---|---|---|
| IAL recording | COR principal attribute (`ial_at_enrollment`); recorded once at canon entry | OrgTree readiness bundle schema |
| AAL recording | COR principal attribute (`aal_required`); declared per principal based on access scope | OrgTree readiness bundle schema |
| AAL drift | `DRIFT-AUTHZ` finding when observed Conditional Access policy state contradicts the COR-recorded AAL | `docs/docs/16_DriftDetectionStandard.qmd` §2 |
| FAL recording | Federation-trust attributes on cross-tenant principals; OrgPath Narrative chapter 10 (Cross-Tenant B2B) covers the operational mechanism | `docs/customer-documents/orgpath-narrative/10-orgpath-and-cross-tenant-collaboration.qmd` |
| Continuous re-verification | Drift Engine evaluates AAL/FAL on every adapter pass; findings emit into the Evidence Bundle | `docs/docs/16_DriftDetectionStandard.qmd` §4 |

The substrate's role in the 800-63 model is **continuous re-verification
of the encoded assurance level**, not initial proofing. Initial proofing
remains the agency's identity-proofing authority's responsibility (a
PIV credentialing authority for most federal civilian agencies).

### 4.1 NIST SP 800-63A — Enrollment and Identity Proofing

NIST SP 800-63A is the volume of the 800-63-3 family that governs the
**enrollment and identity-proofing event** — how an identity is
established before any authenticator is bound to it. For federal
civilian agencies this event happens at the HR system of record at
hire time, months before the principal exists in Entra ID. The
substrate's HRIT inbound provisioning pipeline (UIAO_136 / Spec 2) is
the operational form of NIST SP 800-63A's expectations once the
proofing event has completed and the proofed identity needs to be
expressed as a directory principal.

The substrate is **explicitly not** the identity-proofing authority:
800-63A's IAL-1 / IAL-2 / IAL-3 proofing rigor is the agency's HR /
PIV-issuing authority's responsibility, not the substrate's. What the
substrate does is record the proofed identity's IAL on entry to the
COR and continuously verify that downstream operations don't violate
the assurance level the proofing event established.

| NIST SP 800-63A concept | UIAO substrate component | Canon reference |
|---|---|---|
| Enrollment event (proofed identity established at HR system of record) | HRIT inbound provisioning pipeline normalizes the HR record into the canonical schema; substrate is a consumer of the proofing event, not its source | `Spec2-D1.1` Canonical HR Attribute Schema |
| IAL recording at enrollment | COR principal attribute (`ial_at_enrollment`) populated from the HR record's identity-proofing metadata when the agency's HR adapter exposes it | OrgTree readiness bundle schema |
| Identity-proofing authority chain | `adapter_metadata` per-source provenance bag records which proofing authority asserted the IAL (NFC EmpowHR, Treasury HR Connect, DCPDS, DOI IBC) | `Spec2-D1.1` §`adapter_metadata` |
| Trusted Referee / Supervised Remote (IAL-2/IAL-3 alternates) | Out of substrate scope — agency's HR / PIV authority's responsibility; substrate records the resulting IAL regardless of proofing path | — |
| Re-proofing trigger (substantive identity change) | `Mover` workflow event reflecting a name change or organizational move; substrate routes the change but does not re-proof | `Spec2-D2.2` Mover Workflow |
| Address records (800-63A §4.4 collected attributes) | Optional `addresses` field in the canonical schema; substrate consumes verbatim from HR adapter, does not validate addresses against external authorities | `Spec2-D1.1` §`addresses` |
| Resolution to a single identity (800-63A §4.3) | `employeeId` as immutable correlation anchor (Spec2-D1.1 §3.1); the substrate's resolution is **per HR system**, not cross-system; cross-system resolution remains the HR-platform federation's responsibility | `Spec2-D1.1` §3.1 |

The two surfaces where the substrate's 800-63A treatment becomes
operational:

1. **HRIT inbound provisioning** — covered end-to-end in the
   [Federal HRIT Productization whitepaper](../../whitepapers/federal-hrit-productization.qmd)
   and the
   [OPM HRIT Reference Deployment case study](../../case-studies/reference-deployment-opm-hrit-to-entra.qmd).
   The whitepaper's §5 (Federal mandate alignment) names NIST SP 800-63A
   explicitly; the case study's Phase 1–6 walkthrough shows the
   enrollment event flowing from NFC EmpowHR → canonical schema →
   middleware → OPM Entra under the 800-63A frame.

2. **PIV-derived credential issuance** — covered in
   [§3 NIST SP 800-157](#nist-sp-800-157-derived-piv-credentials)
   above. The 800-63A proofing event upstream of USAccess is the basis
   for the derived-credential trust model 800-157 codifies; the
   substrate's `certificate-anchored: true` invariant binds adapter
   operations to that chain per ADR-052.

Boundary note: an HR system that does not emit identity-proofing
metadata (older NFC / DCPDS connector profiles, for example) cannot
populate `ial_at_enrollment` from the HR side. In that case the
substrate records the IAL at the point the PIV credential is issued
(per the agency's USAccess workflow), and the `DRIFT-PROVENANCE`
finding surfaces if an Entra principal has no recorded IAL after both
upstream paths have run. This is the operational realization of
800-63A's "absence of proofing evidence is itself a finding" posture.

## 5. FICAM Architecture v3

The FICAM Architecture v3 (GSA ICAM Subcommittee) is the federal
reference architecture for identity, credential, and access management.
It describes a federated-governance model with five service domains:
identity management, credential management, access management,
federation, and governance.

UIAO is **not** a FICAM implementation — it is the substrate
**beneath** FICAM that makes the federated-governance model
mechanically verifiable. The relationship parallels the OrgPath
Narrative chapter 07a positioning of UIAO beneath the Azure SSOT
stack: FICAM describes the federal ICAM architecture; UIAO is the open
canon that describes how to keep the architecture's invariants
continuously verified.

| FICAM v3 service domain | UIAO substrate alignment | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Identity Management | COR schema + OrgTree registry + OrgPath attribute | UIAO is the operational form of FICAM's "authoritative identity records" requirement |
| Credential Management | Certificate-anchored adapter operations + PKI adapter contract | UIAO governs the credential's use against canon; the agency's credential issuing authority remains the FICAM credential-management owner |
| Access Management | OrgPath-driven Adaptive Policy Scopes (Purview) + Conditional Access policy state (`DRIFT-AUTHZ`) | The substrate verifies the access decisions FICAM declares are required |
| Federation | OrgPath Narrative chapter 10 (Cross-Tenant B2B); federation-trust attributes on cross-tenant principals | UIAO records federation state; the FAL evaluation under 800-63C remains the agency's federation authority's responsibility |
| Governance | Substrate's six control planes (Identity, Authorization, Compliance, Migration, Evidence, Operational) | UIAO's federated-governance model directly implements FICAM v3's hybrid-governance pattern |

The FICAM v3 Reference Architecture explicitly contemplates that
agencies will compose service offerings from multiple providers. UIAO's
adapter taxonomy (dual-axis: `class` × `mission-class`) is the
substrate-level realization of that composition: each adapter declares
which FICAM service domain it contributes to and which canon documents
its outputs derive from.

## 6. What's NOT in scope here

- **NIST 800-53 Rev 5 control-by-control mapping** — UIAO's KSI library
  at `src/uiao/rules/ksi/` maps to specific 800-53 controls; that
  mapping is documented per-KSI in the YAML rules, not duplicated
  here.
- **NIST 800-171 (CUI) handling** — A.7 of this sub-category covers
  the CUI scope. The substrate's evidence-class invariants do not
  presuppose CUI handling; agencies with CUI requirements add their
  own data-classification overlay.
- **FedRAMP Moderate / High parameter selection** — covered in A.1 and
  A.2 of this sub-category.
- **DoD IL4 / IL5** — A.8 of this sub-category (currently
  future-state).
- **Specific FICAM Playbook implementations** — the FICAM Playbooks
  describe specific deployment patterns (PIV enablement, EICAM
  alignment, etc.). UIAO is the substrate they build on, not a
  Playbook itself.

## 7. Cross-references

- [Federal SSOT Alignment whitepaper](../../whitepapers/federal-ssot-alignment.qmd) — federal mandate landscape (Evidence Act, Federal Data Strategy, CDO, DAMA-DMBOK, Zero Trust, TIC 3.0, NIST 800-63, FedRAMP 20x)
- [Drift Detection Standard](../../../docs/16_DriftDetectionStandard.qmd) — five-class drift taxonomy + §7 Federal SSOT Context + §7.1 SPN drift
- [B.1.1 Three-Way GCC-Moderate Compliance Conflict](../boundary-authorization/B1-1-gcc-moderate-three-way-conflict.qmd) — boundary-side compliance triangle relevant to FedRAMP 20x and TIC 3.0
- [OrgPath Narrative 07a — UIAO Beneath the Azure SSOT Stack](../../orgpath-narrative/07a-uiao-beneath-the-azure-ssot-stack.qmd) — identity-substrate role beneath the Microsoft data SSOT stack
- [Directory Migration README](../../../../src/uiao/modernization/directory-migration/README.md) — discovery and inventory artifacts including the OrgTree readiness bundle
- [AD → Entra ID Reference Deployment Pattern](../../case-studies/reference-deployment-fedciv-ad-to-entra.qmd) — synthetic end-to-end engagement covering the directory-migration dimension
- [Federal HRIT Productization whitepaper](../../whitepapers/federal-hrit-productization.qmd) — customer-facing whitepaper that 800-63A §4.1 above anchors against; covers Spec2-D6.1 / UIAO_136 family
- [OPM HRIT → Entra ID Reference Deployment Pattern](../../case-studies/reference-deployment-opm-hrit-to-entra.qmd) — synthetic end-to-end engagement for the inbound-provisioning dimension; 800-63A enrollment event flows through this case study
- [`Spec2-D1.1` Canonical HR Attribute Schema](../../../../src/uiao/canon/specs/Spec2-D1.1-CanonicalHRAttributeSchema.md) — the schema the HRIT inbound pipeline normalizes records into; `adapter_metadata` carries the per-source 800-63A proofing-authority provenance
- [`adapter-registry.yaml`](../../../../src/uiao/canon/adapter-registry.yaml) — conformance adapter declarations including `certificate-anchored`, `gcc-boundary`, `evidence-class`
- [`modernization-registry.yaml`](../../../../src/uiao/canon/modernization-registry.yaml) — change-making adapter declarations
- [`uiao-control-to-ksi-mapping.yaml`](../../../../src/uiao/rules/ksi/uiao-control-to-ksi-mapping.yaml) — KSI-to-NIST-800-53 control mapping (separate from this cross-walk)

## 8. Provenance

This page is a cross-walk artifact rather than a canonical
specification: every claim above is anchored to a canon document or
schema that is the authoritative source for the assertion. When a
UIAO canon document referenced here is updated, the cross-walk text
in the corresponding row should be re-verified.

The standards cited above are external. Citation policy:

- NIST IR 8011, NIST SP 800-157, NIST SP 800-63 series — referenced
  by document number; consult the current publication revision on the
  NIST CSRC website for authoritative text.
- FICAM Architecture v3 — referenced by version; consult the current
  publication on idmanagement.gov for authoritative text.

This page does not reproduce the standards themselves; it maps UIAO
substrate components to the standards' concepts.
